watch.arrears61plus: {{amount}} in rent is over 60 days overdue — consider initiating a formal debt collection notice.
watch.arrears31to60: {{amount}} in rent is 31–60 days overdue. Send a formal payment reminder to the tenants concerned.
watch.collectionRate: Collection rate is {{rate}}, {{shortfall}} below the 95% target.
watch.vacancy: {{vacantCount}} of {{allUnits}} units are currently vacant ({{rate}} occupancy). Each empty unit represents forgone rental income.
watch.awaitingPayment: {{amount}} in rent invoices for this period are awaiting payment — all expected income has been invoiced but not yet marked paid.
watch.awaitingAndUninvoiced: {{awaitingPayment}} awaiting payment; {{uninvoiced}} in expected rent was not invoiced this period (likely a mid-month move-in).
watch.notInvoiced: {{amount}} in expected rent was not invoiced this period.
watch.buildingsInRedSingle: {{names}} is running at a net loss this period.
watch.buildingsInRedMultiple: {{names}} are running at a net loss this period.
watch.payablesConcentration: {{amount}} in contractor invoices remain unpaid — more than half of this period's expense spend.
watch.expenseSpike: Operating costs are {{pct}} higher than last period — review the expense breakdown to identify the driver.
watch.tenantChurn_one: {{count}} tenant movement this period — check for any gap between a move-out and the next lease start to minimise vacancy loss.
watch.tenantChurn_other: {{count}} tenant movements this period — check for any gap between a move-out and the next lease start to minimise vacancy loss.

note.arrears61plus: {{amount}} in rent was over 60 days overdue at period end.
note.arrears31to60: {{amount}} in rent was 31–60 days overdue at period end.
note.collectionRate: Collection averaged {{rate}} this period — {{shortfall}} below the 95% benchmark.
note.vacancy: Occupancy was {{rate}} — {{vacantCount}} of {{allUnits}} units were unoccupied.
note.awaitingPayment: {{amount}} in rent invoices for this period closed unpaid.
note.awaitingAndUninvoiced: {{awaitingPayment}} closed unpaid; {{uninvoiced}} in expected rent was never invoiced.
note.notInvoiced: {{amount}} in expected rent was not invoiced.
note.payablesConcentration: {{amount}} in contractor invoices remained unpaid at period end.
note.expenseSpike: Operating costs were {{pct}} higher than the prior period.

reason.noiAhead: NOI finished {{pct}}% ahead of the prior year.
reason.noiBehind: NOI finished {{pct}}% below the prior year.
reason.noiOnPar: NOI and collection ({{rate}}) were broadly in line with the prior year.

reason.noiAhead: NOI is up {{pct}}% vs the same period last year.
reason.noiBehind: NOI is down {{pct}}% vs the same period last year.
reason.noiOnPar: NOI and collection ({{rate}}) are in line with last year.

strong.improving: Your portfolio is on a roll.

reason.strongCollectionAllGreen: Collection came in at {{rate}} — all properties in the black.
reason.strongCollectionSomeRed_one: Collection came in at {{rate}} — {{count}} property posted a loss.
reason.strongCollectionSomeRed_other: Collection came in at {{rate}} — {{count}} properties posted losses.
reason.mixedCollection: Collection at {{rate}} — some invoices remain outstanding.
reason.buildingsInRed_one: {{count}} property posted a net loss this period.
reason.buildingsInRed_other: {{count}} properties posted a net loss this period.
reason.hardArrears: {{amount}} in rent arrears is more than 60 days overdue.
reason.negativeNoi: The portfolio posted a net loss of {{amount}}.

allGreen.title: All properties in the green
allGreen.body: Every building in your portfolio posted a positive net result this {{month}}.

strongCollection.title: Strong rent collection
strongCollection.body: {{rate}} of projected rent was collected — well above the 95% benchmark.

positiveNet.title: Positive net result
positiveNet.body: The portfolio generated {{amount}} net income in {{month}} {{year}}.

netIncomeImproved.title: Net income improved
netIncomeImproved.body: Portfolio net result rose by {{amount}} compared to the previous month.

netIncomeDeclined.title: Net income declined
netIncomeDeclined.body: Portfolio net result fell by {{amount}} compared to the previous month.

costsIncreased.title: Maintenance and operating costs increased
costsIncreased.body: Total expenses rose by {{amount}} this month. Review the ledger for the largest contributors.

costsCameDown.title: Operating costs came down
costsCameDown.body: Expenses reduced by {{amount}} vs the prior month.

maintenanceSpend.title: Maintenance spend is active
maintenanceSpend.body_one: {{amount}} in maintenance costs were logged this period across {{count}} building.
maintenanceSpend.body_other: {{amount}} in maintenance costs were logged this period across {{count}} buildings.

noMovements.title: No significant movements this period
noMovements.body: No invoices were issued or paid in the selected month. Ledger activity will appear here once invoices are processed.

insight.noData: No data available for this period.
insight.collectionShortfall: Collection rate was {{rate}}, leaving a {{gap}} gap against the 95% benchmark. Consider reviewing payment terms or tenant communication patterns to close this gap in future periods.
insight.buildingsInRed_one: {{count}} building posted a net loss this period. Recurring maintenance charges may be compressing margins — review service contract costs before the next period.
insight.buildingsInRed_other: {{count}} buildings posted a net loss this period. Recurring maintenance charges may be compressing margins — review service contract costs before the next period.
insight.payablesConcentration: Accrued payables ({{amount}}) represent more than half of total period expenses — contractor cost concentration may warrant a cost review before the next budget cycle.
insight.occupancyChurn_one: {{count}} occupancy change recorded this period. Monitor the effect on collection continuity and vacancy costs in the following month.
insight.occupancyChurn_other: {{count}} occupancy changes recorded this period. Monitor the effect on collection continuity and vacancy costs in the following month.
insight.expenseTrend: Expenses rose by {{pct}} compared to the prior month. If this reflects non-recurring work, no action is needed — if recurring, consider renegotiating maintenance contracts.
insight.normal: Portfolio is performing within normal parameters this period. No structural anomalies detected.

button.continueToSetUp: Continue to set up your property

label.intent: What is your intent for this building? *

explanation.exit_optimizer: You're preparing this property for sale. We'll prioritise decisions that improve presentation and reduce buyer risk, with a short payback horizon.
explanation.yield_maximizer: You want steady, reliable income. We'll favour options that protect cash flow and avoid costly surprises over major upgrade projects.
explanation.value_builder: You're focused on growing the long-term worth of your property. We'll favour investments that extend asset life and improve quality over quick fixes.
explanation.capital_preserver: Stability matters most to you. We'll recommend low-risk, predictable options that avoid large disruptions or uncertain outcomes.
explanation.opportunistic_repositioner: You're ready to invest significantly to reposition this property. We'll favour upgrades with strong long-term upside, even if the upfront cost is higher.

exit_optimizer: We'll prioritise fixes that improve presentation and reduce buyer risk
For repair vs. replace decisions, we'll favour lower upfront cost unless the item directly affects sale readiness
We'll highlight compliance issues that could affect a sale transaction
yield_maximizer: We'll favour reliable, low-disruption maintenance over ambitious upgrades
Recommendations will protect your rental income first — we'll flag anything that risks tenant satisfaction or occupancy
For cashflow planning, we'll lean toward predictable spend and flag surprise-risk items
value_builder: When an asset fails, we'll lean toward replacement if it's past 60% of its useful life rather than patching it
In cashflow planning, we'll flag which investments are worth making now vs. which can wait
Compliance and energy efficiency upgrades will rank higher in our recommendations
capital_preserver: We'll recommend the lowest-risk, most predictable option — repairs over replacements where the risk is manageable
We'll flag any option that introduces cost uncertainty or significant tenant disruption
Large renovation projects will be flagged as low-priority unless compliance requires them
opportunistic_repositioner: We'll look for upgrade opportunities, not just like-for-like replacements
Higher upfront cost is acceptable when the long-term value or rental uplift case is strong
We'll flag modernisation opportunities — energy efficiency, spec upgrades — that align with repositioning

deprioritize.exit_optimizer: We'll deprioritize long-term upgrades with payback beyond your expected sale horizon.
deprioritize.yield_maximizer: We'll deprioritize modernisation projects that disrupt tenants without near-term income impact.
deprioritize.value_builder: We'll deprioritize short-payback cosmetic fixes in favour of durable investments.
deprioritize.capital_preserver: We'll deprioritize any project that introduces cost uncertainty or tenant disruption, even when the long-term upside is real.
deprioritize.opportunistic_repositioner: We'll deprioritize low-impact repairs when a meaningful upgrade option exists.

questions.title: When a major system in your property needs attention, what's your first instinct?
options: Whatever gets it looking good fastest — I may sell soon
Fix it well but don't go overboard — keep rental income flowing
Take the opportunity to upgrade — long-term quality matters
Get it fixed as cheaply as possible — minimal cost, minimal risk
See if this opens up a bigger improvement opportunity

questions.title: How long do you expect to keep this property?
How comfortable are you with larger renovation projects?
How important is it to avoid large surprise expenses?
How much disruption can this property tolerate if the result is better long term?
